exemplar.title:
To what extent did a depreciation in the Pakistani rupee and high rates
of inflation impact imports, exports and growth in Pakistan's pharmaceutical industry from 2022 to 2024?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent did a depreciation in the Pakistani rupee and high rates
of inflation impact imports, exports and growth in Pakistan's pharmaceutical industry from 2022 to 2024?

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question is clearly stated, focused and effectively connected to the discussion.
Topic and scope are communicated accurately throughout the report, with section headings and data displays reinforcing the central focus.
weaknesses: Methodological justification is uneven: reliability checks and rationale for data-frequency choices are only mentioned in passing.
No exploration of alternative methods or deeper justification of selected techniques (e.g., RCA versus other competitiveness metrics).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student communicates the topic and research question with precision and maintains focus throughout, satisfying the top two strands at a high level. However, methodological detail is incomplete, with only partial evidence of informed selection. This aligns the criterion with the 3â4 band; a mark of 4 reflects strong focus and clarity but acknowledges the gaps in methodological rigor.

strengths: Terminology such as âcomparative advantageâ, âinflationary spiralâ and âprice ceilingâ is generally accurate and consistently used.
Source material (IMF, World Bank, SBP, Pacra, peer-reviewed journals) is mostly relevant and applied to illustrate links to the research question.
weaknesses: Several figures and data tables are reproduced descriptively with minimal interrogation of reliability or limitations.
Occasional lapses in labelling and minor conceptual inaccuracies (e.g., swapping âimportsâ and âexportsâ in places) impede coherence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates clear disciplinary knowledge and generally appropriate application of sources, placing it in the 3â4 band. Uneven critical interrogation of source reliability and minor inaccuracies warrant a mid-band mark of 3.

strengths: A broad range of reputable sources is consistently tied to sub-questions, showing attention to relevance.
Analysis often links empirical observations to theory and acknowledges counter-arguments such as political instability and IMF conditions.
weaknesses: Conclusions rely heavily on visual trends without statistical testing or robustness checks.
Many methodological and data-quality limitations (e.g., lack of error bands, undefined concepts, uncited tables) are noted but not fully critiqued or addressed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality and analysis are generally appropriate, and the student constructs a reasoned argument with some critical evaluation, placing the work in the 4â6 band. Multiple weaknesses in methodological rigor and depth of evaluation justify a lower-band mark of 4.

strengths: The report follows the required EE structure with a table of contents, numbered sections, and captions for all figures and tables.
Margins, font sizes and page numbering are consistent throughout.
weaknesses: Residual raw HTML tags, pixelated images and occasional truncation of figure text indicate formatting lapses.
Footnote spacing and some axis-label inconsistencies detract from overall polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the structure and general layout conventions are in place, recurring formatting errors limit the work to the 1â2 band. A mark of 2 reflects the presence of required elements but acknowledges the lack of full coherence and polish.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research questionââTo what extent did a depreciation in the Pakistani rupee and high rates of inflation impact imports, exports and growth in Pakistanâs pharmaceutical industry from 2022 to 2024?ââis prominently stated, unambiguous and appropriately narrow.  Key variables (inflation, exchange rate, imports, exports, growth) and a precise time frame are embedded, giving the essay a measurable focus.

Later analytical sections map explicitly onto these same variables (e.g., RCA index for exports, price-hike graphs for domestic growth proxies), demonstrating an effective connection between question and ensuing discussion.  No evidence of scope-creep or excessive breadth appears.

Given its clarity, measurability and sustained linkage to analysis, the research question satisfies the highest descriptor for clarity and focus.

The student communicates the topic with precision from the title page onward, immediately situating the investigation in Pakistanâs pharmaceutical sector between 2022-24.  A concise contextual paragraph explains the importance of exchange-rate depreciation and inflation, and the purpose of linking these macroeconomic shocks to import, export and growth variables is explicit.

Throughout the report the focus never drifts from this clearly defined scope; each subsection (e.g., 4.1 Drug pricing and shortages, 4.2 Black-market creation) directly addresses the core variables named in the research question.  Section headings, bridging sentences and data displays all reinforce the central line of inquiry, signalling a consistently appropriate and systematic investigation.

Because the topic, purpose and temporal boundaries are expressed accurately and sustained coherently across 32 pages, the work fully meets the descriptors for the top band (5-6).

Data tables, RCA calculations and CPI figures are directly applied to illustrate links between macro shocks and pharmaceutical outcomes, indicating mostly relevant use of material.  Source variety (IMF, World Bank, SBP, Pacra, peer-reviewed journals) strengthens relevance.

Yet some application remains descriptive: several figures are reproduced with minimal critical interrogation, and reliability limitations of key sources (Trading Economics, newspaper articles) are not addressed.  Visual correlations are accepted at face value without statistical testing.

Because the majority of sources are appropriate and their application generally supports the argumentâalbeit with uneven critical depthâthe strand aligns with the 3-4 band.

The essay follows the formal EE structure with an introduction, methodology, analysis, evaluation, conclusion, bibliography and appendix.  Section headings and numbering guide the reader, and tables/figures are captioned.

Yet minor issuesâskipped subsection numbers (4.3), raw HTML artefacts in footnotes and occasional truncation of figure textâdetract from complete coherence.  All required elements are present, but presentation is not flawless.

Therefore the work meets the criteria for a clearly appropriate structure (band 3-4) but earns the lower mark of 3 due to noted inconsistencies.

Margins, font size and page numbers are consistent, and a comprehensive table of contents with hyperlinks is provided.  Figures and tables are generally legible with captions below each visual.

However, residual raw HTML tags, some pixelated images and axis labels that lack unit specificity reduce polish.  Footnote spacing is uneven, indicating minor formatting lapses.

Most layout conventions are applied correctly, satisfying the 3-4 band; a score of 3 reflects the small but recurrent formatting issues.

The student constructs a logical argument: theoretical expectations â empirical findings â evaluation of limitations.  Alternative explanations (political instability, IMF conditions) are acknowledged, showing partially critical evaluation.  Suggestions for supply-side policies indicate forward-looking reflection.

However, evaluation often summarises findings rather than critiquing data reliability or methodological design, and some claims remain unsupported.  The argument structure is reasoned but not fully critical or nuanced.

These qualities align with the 4-6 band, meriting a score of 5.

Analysis links empirical observations to theory (e.g., RCA decline during high CPI, shortage diagram under price ceiling) and often interprets causal mechanisms.  Counter-argumentsâsuch as acknowledging political factors and IMF tariff conditionsâreflect some depth.

Nonetheless, conclusions rely heavily on visual trends without statistical testing or elasticity estimates, and several diagrams are described rather than interrogated.  Assertions about hospital overcrowding or counterfeit drugs lack quantitative backing.

The analysis is clear and largely supported but not yet âeffective and well-supportedâ; hence a mid-band score of 5 (within 4-6) is appropriate.

The essay demonstrates solid command of macroeconomic and trade theory.  Concepts such as cost-push vs demand-pull inflation, revealed comparative advantage and price ceilings are explained accurately and linked to Pakistanâs context.  Historical and institutional knowledge (e.g., DRAP price-setting) reinforces disciplinary understanding.

Minor inaccuracies (e.g., confusing âexportsâ for âimportsâ in isolated sentences) and occasional over-generalisation limit coherence, and deeper theoretical frameworksâfor example, Marshall-Lerner conditionâare not explored.

Overall knowledge is clear and coherent with generally effective source integration, meriting the 3-4 band.

The investigation draws on a broad range of reputable databases (SBP, World Bank), sector studies and regulatory documents, each clearly tied to sub-questions.  Evidence is current (2022-24) and directly relevant to imports, exports and growth metrics.  Multiple data sets are juxtaposed (e.g., inflation with drug-price hikes; exchange rate with RCA), demonstrating sustained attention to relevance.

While methodological limitations exist, the consistency with which sources illuminate the research question exceeds âmajority appropriateâ and approaches âconsistently appropriateâ.  Minor gaps in primary data or robustness checks prevent placement in the top-most (10-12) tier.

Therefore the strand aligns with the 7-9 band and is awarded the lower end, 7.

The student identifies three coherent categories of secondary sourcesâstatistical databases, government/company reports and textbook/online theoryâwith justification of why each was chosen.  The use of HS-code definitions for trade data and explicit mention of cross-referencing signal some informed selection.

However, methodological detail is uneven.  Reliability checks (e.g., triangulation, deflation of nominal data) are only mentioned in passing and the rationale for monthly versus quarterly data is absent.  Primary data collection is dismissed without exploring feasible alternatives, and no explanation is provided for the choice of specific statistical techniques beyond simple visual correlation.

Overall, the range of sources is appropriate and generally relevant, but evidence of systematic, critically informed selection is partial rather than comprehensive, placing the work in the 3-4 band.

Economic terminology such as âshort-run aggregate supplyâ, âcomparative advantageâ, âinflationary spiralâ and âprice ceilingâ is used correctly and consistently within diagrams and prose.  Units and variables are normally labelled precisely (e.g., PKR/USD, RCA formula).

There are sporadic lapsesââincrease in demand-pull inflationâ is phrased awkwardly, and a few axis labels remain genericâbut these do not impede comprehension.

Given mostly accurate and disciplined use of concepts, the strand fits securely in the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The RCA calculation method is introduced but not justified; discussion of alternative trade-competitiveness metrics would strengthen methodological rigor.
Figure 9 effectively reveals an inverse relationship between RCA index and inflation rate, directly addressing the central research question.
Referencing the Oxford IB Economics textbook for theoretical definitions ensures accurate use of economic terminology and concepts.
Figure 1 is described but not interrogated quantitatively; the magnitude of the SRAS shift lacks empirical justification with industry data.
The inflationary spiral diagram concisely captures iterative cost-push and demand-pull effects, reflecting deep conceptual understanding.
Plotting monthly inflation from Jan 2022 to May 2024 allows precise identification of spikes and trends relevant to pharmaceutical pricing.
The inflationary spiral model (Figure 3) is conceptually sound but lacks explicit linkage to pharmaceutical output data, reducing applied relevance.
Figure 4 visually elucidates currency depreciation mechanics, enhancing conceptual clarity for readers.
The student provides a clear word count on the title page, fulfilling the IB requirement and demonstrating adherence to formatting conventions.
The comprehensive, hyperlinked table of contents greatly enhances navigation and reflects effective layout and formatting conventions.
The introduction demonstrates solid disciplinary knowledge by linking political instability and IMF policies to record-high inflation in Pakistan.
The title is prominently stated at the start of the report, immediately situating the investigation within its economic and industrial context.
The table of contents skips subsection 4.3, suggesting inconsistencies in section numbering that may confuse the reader and disrupt structural coherence.
The research question is precisely articulated, embedding key variables and a clear 2022â2024 timeframe to guide a focused analysis.
The contextualisation of pharmaceutical sector significance amid regulatory changes establishes clear relevance and maintains focus on the investigationâs scope.
The rationale for relying solely on secondary sources over primary data is cursory; a more robust justification or exploration of feasible primary methods is needed.
Footnotes placed immediately after text blocks rather than consolidated in endnotes or bibliography disrupt the reading flow and suggest inconsistent citation formatting.
The reliability of key sources like Trading Economics is not critically evaluated, limiting the depth of the economic context presented.
Incorporating exchange-rate analysis alongside inflation broadens scope and shows effective use of source material in trade competitiveness evaluation.
Textual claims about correlation of RCA and exchange rate lack statistical support; inclusion of correlation coefficients would strengthen analysis.
The methodology clearly categorises data sources into statistical databases, government publications, and academic texts, showing structured planning.
Applying the price-ceiling model to illustrate shortages due to DRAP controls successfully bridges theory and observed market outcomes.
The HS-code classification is stated but not critically appraised; discussion of coding limitations or alternate classifications would strengthen data handling.
The cost-push inflation graph effectively links theory to the pharmaceutical context, showing clear understanding of SRAS shifts.
The alternative text for Figure 2 is excessively detailed, repeating full descriptions rather than succinctly summarising analytical insights.
Introducing demand-pull alongside cost-push inflation enriches the theoretical framework and demonstrates accurate use of terminology.
Variables like P_c in the price-ceiling discussion are not formatted in LaTeX and lack precise definition, contravening equation presentation guidelines.
The real exchange rate concept is mentioned but not formally defined or presented with a formula, weakening methodological clarity.
Caption for Figure 6 references data from 2018â2023, conflicting with fiscal-year labels and reducing clarity of the time frame.
The acknowledgement of data availability challenges for recent years shows analytical reflection and personal engagement with research limitations.
Initiating findings with a data-driven inflation trend indicates strong relevance of research to the central question.
The assertion that Pakistan uniquely regulates all drug prices lacks citation, undermining the credibility of this policy claim.
The inflation trend graph (Figure 5) omits error bands or confidence intervals, obscuring potential data uncertainty.
Figure 6âs comparison of drug price hikes and inflation effectively supports the analysis of regulatory impacts on pricing.
Repeated numbering of Figure 7 for both the supply-demand graph and later figure causes confusion; consistent figure numbering is needed.
Linking black-market price data to patient access underscores the societal impact, demonstrating strong engagement with real-world issues.
Table 1 lacks source citations for black-market price figures, raising questions about data provenance and research quality.
The bibliography demonstrates a broad and reputable range of sources, indicating substantial research depth and disciplinary engagement.
A dual-axis presentation may mislead; statistical measures of correlation or separate standardized axes would clarify the RCA-inflation relationship.
Discussion of political instability remains descriptive; a critical assessment of policy outcomes and metrics is needed for depth.
Evaluating IMF bailout measures and healthcare funding reflects analytical selfâawareness and analytical reflection on limiting factors.
The evaluation section is not clearly numbered or subdivided, reducing navigational clarity and structural consistency.
Proposed case studies are noted but lack methodological outline; specifying data sources and analysis frameworks would improve feasibility.
Concluding policy recommendations on supply-side measures are forward-looking and demonstrate the studentâs capacity for critical evaluation.
Claims about hospital overcrowding lack citation or quantitative evidence, reducing the credibility of healthcare impact statements.
Some URLs include access dates while others omit them, reducing consistency and completeness in source documentation.
Bibliography entries exhibit inconsistent formatting of titles, italics, and punctuation, deviating from a single citation style.
